reasoning: Query is in Thai (th) and requests budget accommodation ≤5000 yen/night in Kyoto. Tool returns Kyoto hotels in Thai (lang param respected) — RULE D check: lang=th was passed so no language mismatch. However no price/budget filtering exists; response includes DoubleTree Hilton properties that are well above 5000 yen/night. The budget constraint is the primary failure (RULE B: single root cause). Data is real OSM/Wikidata hotel records — groundedness 4. Specificity partial: coordinates and phone present for some, but no price_band or website for most.
missing_data: Price band / rate data for budget filtering; hostel or guesthouse type lodging; sorted by budget tier
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type filter (hostel/guesthouse) and price_band=budget filtering; surface capsule hotels and guest houses first for budget queries

reasoning: Query is in German asking for free things to do in Tokyo on a small budget. Tool returns get_spots Tokyo in English (lang not set to 'de') — RULE D applies: language mismatch. Constraint failure is the dominant issue per RULE B. Data quality is good (real Wikidata spots with price_band fields), and some spots shown like Eitai Bridge and Mount Takao do have price_band=free. However the free-entry filter was not applied and the first result (Hamarikyu Gardens) has fee=yes, weakening practical utility. Partial intent match — some useful free spots present but not filtered.
missing_data: German language response; filter for price_band=free only; explicit free-entry flag shown prominently
improvement_hint: Apply lang=de and filter by price_band=free when query contains budget/free keywords; surface free parks and temples first

reasoning: Query asks for free-entry temples/shrines in Kamakura specifically. The hybrid search returns results scattered across Kanagawa, Fukui, etc. — very few are actually in Kamakura. The top result (旅のプランニング) is a navigation/planning page about nearby temples including 浄智寺 in Kamakura but is not a direct spot. Second and third results are from Fukui (猫寺, 永平寺). Only one result partially addresses Kamakura. Retrieval failure: the geographic constraint (Kamakura) was not filtered properly, and free-entry constraint was ignored. RULE B: retrieval is the single worst root cause.
missing_data: Kamakura-specific temples with free entry such as Tsurugaoka Hachimangu, Engakuji grounds, Zuisenji; price_band=free filter
improvement_hint: Add municipality filter for Kamakura (鎌倉市) combined with price_band=free; use get_spots with municipality rather than full-text hybrid search for location-specific queries

reasoning: Query asks specifically about Fukuoka yatai (street food stalls) under ¥500 per meal. get_local_food returns GI-designated food products (八女伝統本玉露 tea, ふぐ, はかた地どり) and scraped regional food — none of these are yatai or budget dining venues. Yatai are a specific Fukuoka experience with actual stalls (中洲, 天神 etc.) and no price info is returned. This is a retrieval failure: the tool class is wrong for live venue/dining queries, and ¥500 price constraint is completely unaddressed. RULE A: wrong tool but score actual data returned — data is authoritative GI food records, just off-topic. Scored 2 not 1 on intent because Fukuoka food dimension is technically present.
missing_data: Yatai stall information (中洲、天神エリア), price per meal data, operating hours/locations for street food stalls
wrong_data: Cherry Blossoms in Fukuoka entry is scraped noise — not a food item
improvement_hint: Route yatai/屋台 queries to search_hybrid or get_spots with kind=food_market; add yatai as a dedicated kind in the taxonomy

reasoning: Query in Chinese (zh) asks for free-entry shrines or temples in Kyoto. Tool passes lang=zh and returns Kyoto shrines — language respected, RULE D not triggered. However: (1) free-entry constraint is completely unaddressed — no price_band=free filter, (2) returned results are mostly obscure rural shrines (相楽神社, 荒見神社 etc.) from Kyoto Prefecture but not Kyoto City, (3) results lack descriptions in Chinese (description_en is null for most). Single worst failure is the free constraint (RULE B: F). Data is real Wikidata records so groundedness is fine. Specificity is low — no addresses, visiting info, or fee data.
missing_data: Free-entry filter (price_band=free); well-known Kyoto shrines like Fushimi Inari (free), Yasaka Jinja (free); Chinese descriptions; entry fee info
improvement_hint: Apply price_band=free filter for 免費/無料 queries; prioritize municipality=Kyoto City spots over rural Kyoto Prefecture shrines

reasoning: Query asks for cheap eats in Sapporo — local markets and affordable restaurants under ¥1,500. Tool returns get_local_food for all of Hokkaido, yielding MAFF GI products (Yubari Melon, Tokachi yam, potato varieties, dried sea cucumber) — these are agricultural GI designations, not dining venues or market stalls. No restaurant, market, or price-per-meal data present. Retrieval failure: wrong data layer for a dining-venue query. Query is for Sapporo specifically; response is all-Hokkaido. Per RULE A, the tool choice yields authoritative food records but entirely off-topic for the dining intent.
missing_data: Sapporo Nijo Market, Curb Market info, ramen shops, affordable restaurant venues, price-per-meal data
improvement_hint: For cheap-eats queries, route to search_hybrid or get_spots with kind=food_market/restaurant filter; add Sapporo city parameter to narrow scope

reasoning: Query in Indonesian asks for cheap hostels in Osaka under ¥3,000/night. Tool passes lang=id and returns Osaka hotels — but no Indonesian translations (name_zh/ko null, name_en sparse), and no budget/price filtering. Response includes ANA Crowne Plaza and DoubleTree Hilton — luxury hotels far above ¥3,000. Lodging_type=hostel not filtered. Per RULE D: no explicit language mismatch since lang=id was passed even though data lacks Indonesian text. Budget constraint is the main failure (RULE B: F). Some basic hotel records with coordinates exist so groundedness is 4.
missing_data: Hostel/guesthouse type filter; budget price_band; Indonesian language descriptions; properties under ¥3,000/night
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type=hostel filter and price_band=budget for hostel murah/cheap hostel queries; suppress luxury chains from budget searches

reasoning: Query asks about buying cheap souvenirs at 100-yen shops and supermarkets. Tool returns get_local_specialty for Aichi (food category) yielding only 3 GI-designated agricultural products (八丁味噌, 冬瓜, 紫蘇花穂). This is completely off-topic: the user wants retail shopping locations (100-yen shops, supermarkets) where they can buy affordable souvenirs. The prefecture (Aichi) was not mentioned in the query — the tool was called for an arbitrary prefecture. GI products are authoritative but entirely irrelevant to the shopping/souvenir intent. RULE A: wrong tool class. Score intent=1 because no aspect of the query is addressed.
missing_data: 100-yen shop locations, supermarket souvenir recommendations, affordable omiyage suggestions, shopping district info
improvement_hint: Route budget souvenir/shopping queries to search_hybrid with shopping/omiyage keywords; the MCP server does not cover retail pricing — clarify this scope limitation

reasoning: Query asks for best free outdoor parks and nature spots across Japan (no location specified, free entry). search_hybrid with 公園 無料 自然 returns a mix of real spots including 浮間公園 (Tokyo), 荒川自然公園, 西山公園 Fukui (explicitly free per description). Coverage is nationwide — geographically reasonable. However, the free-entry constraint is only partially met: some results mention 無料 in text but no systematic price_band=free filtering. Some results are noisy (光市観光協会 website page, not a specific spot; みよし市 government category page). Score intent=3 per RULE C — partial match with real data but scope/filter gap. Constraint failure is primary (no fee=no filter).
missing_data: price_band=free filter applied systematically; better deduplication of scrape results that are site-level pages rather than individual spots
improvement_hint: Apply fee=no / price_band=free filter for free-entry queries; filter out non-spot scrape results (category/navigation pages)

reasoning: Query is in Korean asking for free parks and outdoor spots in Tokyo. Tool returns get_spots Tokyo without lang=ko — RULE D applies: language mismatch (response returns en/ja names, not ko). However name_ko fields ARE populated in many records (하마리큐 은사정원, 에이타이교, 다카오산 etc.), so ko translations are partially available. The free-entry constraint is not filtered: first result is Hamarikyu Gardens with fee=yes. Spots include price_band=free items (Eitai Bridge, Mt Takao) but mixed with paid ones. RULE B: constraint failure is the single worst dim (free filter not applied, language mismatch secondary). Per RULE D: drop constraint_handling by 1.
missing_data: Korean language response (lang=ko param missing); price_band=free filter; explicit free parks like Shinjuku Gyoen outer garden, Ueno Park
improvement_hint: Pass lang=ko when query is in Korean; add price_band=free filter for 무료/입장료 없이 queries

reasoning: Query is in Arabic asking for cheapest/free tourist spots in Nagano. Tool returns get_spots Nagano in English (no lang=ar). RULE D: language mismatch — drop constraint_handling by 1. Data is real authoritative Wikidata spots for Nagano. Some spots have price_band data: 龍岡城 has price_band=low, 松代城 has fee=no/price_band=free. But no systematic free-entry filter is applied and Kamikochi (no price_band set) appears first. Partial coverage of the free/cheap intent. RULE C: authoritative data with filter gap = 3. Single worst failure is the free constraint + language (RULE B: F).
missing_data: Arabic language response; price_band=free filter applied; more free spots like Zenkoji temple grounds access areas, free viewing platforms
improvement_hint: Pass lang=ar for Arabic queries; apply price_band=free/low filter for cheap-spots queries; surface spots with explicit fee=no prominently

reasoning: Query asks for luxury honeymoon onsen ryokan in Hokkaido with no budget limit. Tool returns get_hotels Hokkaido in English. Response includes several 日本秘湯を守る会 onsen ryokan (知内温泉旅館, 銀婚湯, 湯元 凌雲閣, 湯元 湧駒荘) — these are genuine onsen ryokan from an official Japanese secret hot springs association. This is a good partial match for the intent. However, luxury tier properties like Zaborin, Hoshino Resorts Tomamu, or Niseko luxury lodges are absent. No room type/amenity data (private onsen baths, kaiseki dining) is present. The onsen_ryokan lodging_type and 日本秘湯 designation are partially relevant but the luxury constraint is not well-served — ranking failure (luxury properties buried or absent). Coordinates are null for ryokan entries, limiting feasibility assessment.
missing_data: Luxury tier ryokan properties; private onsen room data; kaiseki dining info; property websites and pricing tier
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type=onsen_ryokan filter and surface hito_yu_kai / known luxury brands first; include ryokan with website links for luxury queries

reasoning: Query in Japanese asks for luxury Kyoto ryokan for honeymoon with kaiseki cuisine and private outdoor bath rooms. Tool returns all Kyoto hotels without ryokan filter — response starts with AB Hotel, APA Garden Court, Comfort INN (budget/mid-range business hotels), followed by DoubleTree properties. No traditional ryokan with kaiseki/露天風呂 appear in the visible truncated section. Luxury constraint completely unaddressed. Lang=ja is respected. This mirrors R-276 pattern: get_hotels without lodging_type=ryokan filter returns inappropriate results. Constraint failure is the dominant issue (RULE B: F). Data is real and authoritative but the wrong tier/type.
missing_data: onsen_ryokan type filter; luxury ryokan options in Kyoto like 柊家, 炭屋, 俵屋; kaiseki and private onsen room attributes
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type=onsen_ryokan filter for 旅館/ryokan queries; distinguish luxury tier from business hotels; expose amenity fields (kaiseki, rotenburo)
